content.problemStatement:
You are given a square grid GGG with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN). Let's denote the element in the iii-th row and jjj-th column by Gi,jG_{i, j}Gi,jâ€‹. Initially, for each valid i,ji, ji,j, the element Gi,jG_{i, j}Gi,jâ€‹ is equal to iâ‹…N+ji \cdot N+jiâ‹…N+j.

The grid is changing in steps. In one step, either one row or one column of the grid is selected uniformly randomly and then, all the elements in the selected row or column are shuffled uniformly randomly.

For a given cell in row rrr and column ccc, find the probability that after KKK steps, Gr,cG_{r, c}Gr,câ€‹ is equal to râ‹…N+cr \cdot N + crâ‹…N+c. It can be proved that this probability can be expressed as an irreducible fraction p/qp/qp/q, where qqq is a positive integer coprime to 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353. You should compute pâ‹…qâˆ’1p \cdot q^{-1}pâ‹…qâˆ’1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, where qâˆ’1q^{-1}qâˆ’1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of qqq modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers NNN, KKK, rrr and ccc.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer pâ‹…qâˆ’1â€‰%â€‰998,244,353p \cdot q^{-1}\, \%\, 998,244,353pâ‹…qâˆ’1%998,244,353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Since there is only one row and one column, both with just one element, the grid after each step is the same as the initial grid.
